Founder

Electrical engineer with a commercial operator mindset

I bridge electrical engineering, venture strategy, technical systems thinking, and operational realism — because infrastructure startups die at the seams between all of those things.

Trained as an Electrical and Electronics Engineer, I now operate at the intersection of engineering, commercialization, and venture building. My approach isn't presentation-first. It starts from site reality, economics, risk, reliability, and the discipline required to make deployment actually repeatable.

Venture track: ZephyrFuel — on-site industrial hydrogen under a build-own-operate model.
Software track: Sherpa — operating intelligence for physical facility nodes.
AI track: ARGHMS — railway hazard detection and risk analysis pipelines.
Engineering track: B.Tech in Electrical & Electronics, rooftop solar feasibility, and LV/HV design coordination.
Commercial track: MBA, Markstrat, customer discovery, financial modeling, and venture strategy.
Featured work

Venture and operator portfolio

The common pattern: find the constraint that blocks deployment or adoption, model the economics, build the operating path, then convert discovery into execution.

⛽
↗

ZephyrFuel

On-site industrial hydrogen

A build-own-operate model for industrial sites that need reliable hydrogen without owning complex production assets.

✓Targets industrial sites exposed to delivered hydrogen volatility, logistics risk, and uptime pressure.
✓Frames hydrogen as a system: uptime, power, water, storage, service, and contracted supply.
✓Customer discovery, unit economics, and project-finance logic to test deployment readiness.
🧠
↗

Sherpa

Operating intelligence

Sherpa is the brain for physical infrastructure nodes. In ZephyrFuel, facility nodes are the muscle; Sherpa coordinates performance, risk, maintenance, and visibility across them.

✓Connects plant nodes into one operating intelligence layer.
✓Tracks production logs, power, water quality, asset health, maintenance, and availability.
✓Turns deployment data into operating learning that improves every site that follows.
🚆
↗

ARGHMS

AI infrastructure prototype

A technical systems project for railroad ground hazard detection using video processing, pose estimation, and risk classification.

✓GPU-optimized processing pipelines for railway video datasets.
✓Object detection, pose estimation, and contextual risk scoring.
✓Structured for scalable annotation, model training, and operational dashboards.

Unmanned Disaster Management Surveillance Machine

Prototype concept

First-responder prototype concept inspired by the Tham Luang cave rescue — designed for rapid assessment in constrained-access disaster environments.

✓System objectives, constraints, and architecture for tight-access emergency settings.
Video 1 ↗ Video 2 ↗
Project media

Prototype videos

Documentation footage from the Unmanned Disaster Management Surveillance Machine concept — constrained-access emergency response system design.

▶

Prototype Demo — Physical Build

The actual robot: PVC-frame chassis with independent suspension, coloured-wheel drive train, and onboard sensor module for field navigation in constrained environments.

Watch on YouTube ↗
▶

Prototype Demo — Control & Sensor Dashboard

Live telemetry interface showing temperature, humidity, pressure, altitude (64.8 m), air quality, and battery — streamed from the robot in real time during field operation.

Watch on YouTube ↗
Systems architecture

Software becomes useful when it controls real operating complexity

The central idea: physical nodes do the work, operating intelligence coordinates the network, and every deployment improves the next one.

🏭

Physical nodes

Facility-level assets handle the real work — power, water, production, maintenance, inventory, labor, testing, and delivery constraints.

🧠

Operating intelligence

Sherpa logic

Monitors, learns, flags risk, supports troubleshooting, and turns operating data into deployment memory.

🔗

Deployment network

As more sites come online, operating data improves maintenance logic, underwriting confidence, delivery promises, and replication speed.

Capstone consulting

Hyundai Rotem Riverside facility project

A team-based operations consulting engagement — production flow, KPI visibility, supply-chain timing risk, and practical process redesign at a rail electronics facility serving LA Metro delivery needs.

Hyundai Rotem Riverside — Spring 2026 MBA capstone consulting team visit.

Process flow

Mapped how components move from receiving through assembly, WIP, testing, and shipment readiness.

Supply risk

Built questions around Korean supplier dependency, lead-time variability, and safety-stock logic.

KPI visibility

Scoped a minimum viable control tower for schedule, inventory, supplier OTIF, and delay logs.

Implementation roadmap

Focused the engagement on changes feasible within an 8-week consulting window.

Operating edge

Commercial mind + technical execution

Useful infrastructure work needs both: the field reality of utilities, equipment, permits, and operations — and the tools that compress discovery and deployment cycles.

⚡

Energy economics

Power tariffs, PPAs, delivered hydrogen benchmarks, incentives, and project-level underwriting.

Compression limits, uptime, maintenance, water quality, storage behavior, and site integration.

OpenCV, YOLO, CUDA, dataset scaling, dashboards, structured storage, and ML pipelines.
